title: [Bug]: open-release-pr explicit issues should be written into the PR body
summary: The --issue arguments used for label synchronization are not currently written into the generated pull request body as Related issue references.
observed: A maintainer can pass --issue 209 and labels are copied, but the generated PR body still contains only general instructions instead of a concrete Related issue line.
expected: Explicit issue numbers should appear in a dedicated Related Issues section so reviewers can see the managed issue links without inferring them from labels or branch names.
reproduction: Run scripts/open-release-pr.sh with --issue NUMBER and inspect the generated PR body.
failing_test: Add a shell-helper regression test proving explicit issue arguments are rendered into the PR body.
impact: Review traceability is weaker and source issue detection relies more heavily on command arguments. Runtime package behavior is unaffected.
delivery_semantics: This bug is limited to GitHub workflow metadata automation and cannot affect message delivery semantics.
security: Only numeric issue references should be written. The helper must not copy free-form private text, endpoints, or payloads into the PR body.
acceptance: Explicit --issue values are rendered as Related issue lines in the PR body.
Multiple explicit issues are rendered deterministically.
The generated instructions no longer contain a literal numeric placeholder issue.
A fake-gh shell regression covers the behavior.
tests: Run python -m pytest tests/unit/test_pr_auto_approval.py tests/unit/test_pr_label_sync.py -q and scripts/check.sh.
documentation: Update workflow documentation only if command examples need clarification.
closeout: Close after the bug branch merges into the release branch and release-gated closure confirms the release has shipped.
